race.name: GFNY Aguascalientes
race.tagline: BE A PRO FOR A DAY in Mexico's cycling hotbed with views of Cerro del Muerto

climate.overview: Warm, welcoming highland climate with thermal baths; named for 'hot waters'[1][3]

terrain.overview: Rolling hills with max gradient 6.3%, quiet roads, cactus trees, desert landscapes, and constant views of Cerro del Muerto mountain range[1]

logistics.explanation: AGU airport 20min from hotels with direct US flights; drivable from major Mexican cities[1][3]

length.explanation: Solid 145km/90mi with 1500m+ climbing for GFNY franchise event[1]

technicality.explanation: Rolling terrain max 6.3%; no extreme technical demands mentioned[1]

elevation.explanation: 1511m gain over 145km = moderate for distance (1% avg gradient)[1]

climate.explanation: Warm March highland weather + thermal bath recovery[1][3]

altitude.explanation: Aguascalientes ~1880m; noticeable but not extreme high altitude[3]

adventure.explanation: Desert roads, Cerro del Muerto views, cultural immersion, vineyards[1][3]

prestige.explanation: GFNY franchise event pattern; new but established global brand[1][2]

race_quality.explanation: GFNY World Series standards with pro-am format and international timing[1][2][3]

experience.explanation: Post-race: birria, carne asada, thermal baths, La Saturnina, vineyards[1][3]

community.explanation: 'Hotbed for cycling in Mexico' per GFNY CEO; HQ team homecoming[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Inaugural event; GFNY typically attracts strong international fields[1]

value.explanation: Pricing unavailable but GFNY franchise + Mexico destination suggests good value

expenses.explanation: Affordable Mexico destination; direct US flights but international travel varies[1][3]

biased_opinion.overview: GFNY's Mexico expansion hits with a culturally rich, scenic highland challenge. El Ocote climb and thermal bath recovery make this inaugural event instantly bucket-list worthy for LatAm endurance cyclists.

logistics.overview: Aguascalientes Airport (AGU) 20min from hotels. Direct flights: LAX(4h), HOU(2.5-3h), MIA(4h), BOG(5-6h). National: TIJ(3h), CUN(2h). Drive: Mexico City(4-5h), Guadalajara(2-3h)[1][3]

history.overview: Inaugural event March 22, 2026. Announced Dec 2025 as GFNY HQ team 'homecoming' in Mexico's cycling hotbed[1][2]

course_description.character: Starts/finishes Isla San Marcos. Rolling hills w/ 6.3% max gradient, 4 notable climbs. **Suffering Zone 1: El Ocote** - 5km early climb w/ rock climbing range vistas. Quiet roads + Cerro del Muerto views after[1]

non_negotiables: Book early for GFNY World Series qualifier corral eligibility
Reserve thermal baths for post-race recovery
Sample birria + guavas at La Saturnina
